start_cue: Three full-body sessions a week, about two days apart. Monday, Wednesday, Friday is the cleanest default. Show up before you optimize.

weeks.focus: 3 sessions/week full-body at 3–4 reps in reserve
weeks.detail: Each session covers the major patterns: a squat or leg-press variant and a hip hinge as lower-body foundation, then the aesthetic priority — horizontal push, horizontal pull, vertical pull, lateral raise, and one arm movement alternated between biceps and triceps. 2–3 working sets per exercise. Train at 3–4 RIR — sets that could clearly have gone further. Bias your best energy toward the upper-body work; the leg patterns are in for base and injury-proofing, not for the appearance lever.

Keep the three-session full-body layout. Start pushing closer to failure on the main movements of each session. Add a rep or the smallest load increment each week you complete all sets with clean form. Some weeks the linear progression stalls on one or two exercises — that is expected, not a signal to change the program.

weeks.focus: Hold 2 RIR on most sets; watch for progression stalls
weeks.detail: Working at 2 RIR on most sets is appropriate by this point. Linear progression will begin to stall on the compounds somewhere in this window — that is the signal the beginner block has done its job.

graduation: When weekly progression has stalled on the main compounds, sessions feel sustainable rather than exhausting, and you can consistently judge what 2 RIR feels like, move to the 5-day split and the main framework. If any of those are not yet true, stay in the beginner block another month.
